title: Reinforcing Loops

content.question: What's your strongest compounding loop, and are you feeding or neglecting it?
sop: Identify your strongest current reinforcing loop, such as referrals, reviews, content, or repeat buyers.
Set the Base and Rate you're currently feeding that loop with (see calculation, page 5).
Remove one interruption that's currently breaking the loop's momentum.
Invest disproportionately in feeding that loop over starting a new one.
Track its growth monthly to confirm it's actually compounding, not just staying flat.
questions: What's your strongest compounding loop, and are you feeding or neglecting it?
Which of your current loops, referrals, reviews, repeat buyers, is actually accelerating versus just staying flat?
What's interrupting your best loop right now that you could remove this week?

definitions.def: A feedback loop where success creates more success.

content.equation_example: Base 10 referral-driven customers, Rate 20% month-over-month growth in that loop, Time 6 months. Growth equals 10 times 1.20 to the 6th power, approximately 30. A higher rate or longer time means the loop is reinforcing strongly, small early feeding produces large later gains; a low or zero rate means the loop has stalled and isn't compounding on its own anymore.
content.closing_statement: Feed what's already compounding before starting something new.

caption:
Principle Nº 121: Reinforcing Loops

What grows, grows faster.

Ask yourself: What's your strongest compounding loop, and are you feeding or neglecting it?
